cards.front: What does the -R option do in the chmod command?
cards.back: Recursively changes the file permissions for all files and directories in the specified directory

cards.front: What is the default permission value for a new file?
cards.back: The owner has read, write, and execute permissions, the group has read and execute permissions, and others have read and execute permissions

cards.front: What is the purpose of the ugo-rwx permissions?
cards.back: The ugo-rwx permissions represent the permissions for the owner, group, and others respectively

cards.front: What is the difference between chmod and chown?
cards.back: chmod changes the file permissions, while chown changes the owner of a file

cards.front: What is the effect of using chmod 777 on a file?
cards.back: The file will have read, write, and execute permissions for all users (owner, group, and others)
